title: Fix Google Client ID configuration error

requirements.summary: Configure VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID environment variable to resolve OAuth connection error
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Connect Google Account' button functions without showing the configuration error
The VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID environment variable is properly loaded and accessible
Users can successfully initiate the Google OAuth flow

file_operations.description: Update the .env.example template to provide clearer instructions for obtaining the Google OAuth Client ID from Google Cloud Console. Include step-by-step guidance for: 1) Creating OAuth credentials in Google Cloud Console, 2) Configuring authorized JavaScript origins and redirect URIs, 3) Copying the Client ID into the .env file. Ensure the VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID variable is clearly documented with the exact format expected.

Add runtime validation to check if VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is properly configured before initiating OAuth flow. Improve error handling to provide actionable feedback when the environment variable is missing or empty. Verify the environment variable is accessed correctly using import.meta.env.VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID syntax. Add console logging to help diagnose configuration issues during development.

Enhance the error display to show more specific guidance when VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is not configured. Update the UI to display the current configuration error message more prominently with instructions linking to the .env.example file. Ensure the 'Connect Google Account' button is disabled when the Client ID is missing, with a tooltip explaining the configuration requirement.

requirements.summary: Verify end-to-end Google OAuth flow functionality after Client ID configuration
acceptanceCriteria: Users can complete the Google OAuth authorization flow
Connected Google accounts display correctly in the UI
Users can successfully post video clips to YouTube after connecting

file_operations.description: Add comprehensive error handling and validation for the OAuth callback flow. Verify that the authorization code is properly extracted from URL parameters, state parameter is validated, and error responses from Google are handled gracefully. Add user-friendly error messages for common OAuth failure scenarios (user cancellation, invalid state, expired tokens). Ensure proper redirection to the main app with success/failure status displayed via toast notifications.

Verify the OAuth flow constructs the correct authorization URL with required scopes (youtube.upload, youtube.readonly). Ensure the redirect URI matches the authorized URI configured in Google Cloud Console. Add validation to confirm the state parameter is properly generated and stored for CSRF protection. Test that the connection status updates correctly after successful OAuth completion.

Ensure connected Google/YouTube account information displays correctly after successful OAuth flow, including channel name and avatar. Verify the disconnect functionality properly clears OAuth credentials and updates UI state. Add loading states during connection/disconnection operations. Test that the component correctly reflects connection status from useQueries hooks (useHasGoogleOAuthCredentials, useIsYouTubeChannelConnected).

Verify the YouTube posting flow validates OAuth connection status before attempting to post clips. Ensure proper error handling for authentication failures (expired tokens, revoked access). Add validation that clips meet YouTube Shorts requirements (max 60 seconds). Test that success/error responses are properly displayed to users via toast notifications with actionable messages.
